title: Flash Report on OHCHR mission to Bangladesh: interviews with Rohingyas fleeing from Myanmar since 9 October 2016
author_or_org: Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights
geographic_scope: Northern Rakhine State, especially north Maungdaw
strengths: Early UN fieldwork with refugee interviews; precise displacement figures; strong qualitative evidence on killing patterns and theater footprint.
limitations: No hard civilian death total beyond 'hundreds'; limited direct access inside Myanmar.
notes: Best source for the 2016 security-operation zone and forced-displacement scale.

title: How a two-week army crackdown reignited Myanmar's Rohingya crisis
pages_or_sections: main text; lines around village sweep, death estimates, and troop count
strengths: Best reconstruction of the late-November 2016 sweep; gives a specific village-cluster footprint and a soldier-count estimate.
limitations: Civilian death estimate relies partly on Rohingya elders; not a named-victim dataset.
notes: Key source for proposing the two-week northwest Maungdaw candidate episode.

title: Exclusive: More than 1,000 feared killed in Myanmar army crackdown on Rohingya - U.N. officials
geographic_scope: Northern Rakhine State / refugee outflow into Bangladesh
strengths: Preserves the higher U.N.-official concern that 2016 death totals exceeded 'hundreds'.
limitations: Inference from refugee reports rather than a direct named-victim count; not tightly sub-episoded.
notes: Useful mainly as a high-end 2016 contextual bound.

title: Burma: New Wave of Destruction in Rohingya Villages
strengths: Records the government's own casualty line while also emphasizing verification gaps and widespread abuses.
limitations: Does not independently resolve militant/civilian status for the official dead.
notes: Important for preserving the official 69 alleged militants / 17 security-force deaths claim without endorsing it.

title: Situation in the People's Republic of Bangladesh/Republic of the Union of Myanmar: Request for authorisation of an investigation pursuant to article 15
author_or_org: Office of the Prosecutor, International Criminal Court
geographic_scope: Northern Rakhine State and Bangladesh displacement context
strengths: Synthesizes multiple high-quality evidentiary records; explicit on three-township geography and state-force scale.
limitations: Not itself a primary casualty registry; some numeric statements are ceilings or summaries from other sources.

title: The statistics from our Rohingya report are sickening
author_or_org: Doctors Without Borders / Médecins Sans Frontières
pages_or_sections: main text summarizing retrospective mortality survey
geographic_scope: Rohingya from northern Rakhine interviewed in refugee settlements in Bangladesh
strengths: Tight one-month window and explicit violent/nonviolent split; strongest direct-death floor for the acute 2017 episode.
limitations: Refugee-camp survey does not cover families who never reached Bangladesh; upper bound not pinned down.
notes: Core civilian numerator source for the acute 2017 episode.

citation_short: Parmar et al. / PHR mixed-method study (2019)
title: Qualitative evidence of crimes against humanity: the August 2017 attacks on the Rohingya in northern Rakhine State, Myanmar
author_or_org: Parmar, Messner, et al. / Physicians for Human Rights collaborators
strengths: Near-complete hamlet/ward coverage of affected Rohingya localities across the three-township belt; useful for baseline civilian exposure.
limitations: 7803 death estimate is mixed violent/nonviolent and extends beyond the acute first month.
notes: Best denominator anchor for exposed Rohingya population.

title: Special Report: The shock troops who expelled the Rohingya from Myanmar
geographic_scope: Northern Rakhine State, especially Maungdaw and Rathedaung
strengths: Detailed reconstruction of state-force units and operational footprint.
limitations: Official ARSA death numbers remain status-contested and may embed misclassification.
notes: Key source for attacker identity and official combatant-death claims.

title: At least 71 killed in Myanmar as Rohingya insurgents stage major attack
strengths: Same-day casualty reporting tied directly to the outpost assaults.
limitations: Official early figure; still vulnerable to later revision and status disputes.
notes: Useful for a narrow case-specific combatant sensitivity because it is tied to an actual armed assault.

title: Myanmar: New evidence reveals Rohingya armed group massacred scores in Rakhine State
geographic_scope: Northern Maungdaw / Kha Maung Seik cluster and Myo Thu Gyi area
strengths: Important side-attributed counter-series showing ARSA civilian killings and noting ARSA's 'core group' was only in the hundreds.
limitations: Not a full theater-wide civilian-death dataset; focused on particular incidents and communities.
notes: Used to preserve attacker-side complexity and to constrain ARSA denominator assumptions.

title: Myanmar Situation: Operational Update on UNHCR's emergency response in Bangladesh
strengths: Useful dated displacement checkpoint during the acute episode.
limitations: Operational summary rather than a methodological population study.
notes: Used only as an outflow anchor, not as a full exposed-population estimate.

title: ISCG Situation Report: Cox's Bazar Influx, 24 September 2017
strengths: Anchors the end-of-window refugee count for the acute one-month episode.
limitations: Operational summary; not a full uncertainty model.
notes: Used together with the 12 September UNHCR checkpoint to bound civilian person-months.
